sections.title: Identity and Access (Azure AD / Entra ID)

items.title: MFA enforced for privileged roles
items.description: Require MFA for Global Admin, Security Admin, and privileged roles.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether privileged directory roles require MFA. Review Conditional Access and role assignments:

```
az rest --method GET --url "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies" --query "value[].{name:displayName,state:state}"
az role assignment list --all --query "[?roleDefinitionName=='Global Administrator'].principalName"
```

Privileged roles without an MFA-enforcing CA policy = critical. Automated: Prowler/ScoutSuite.
items.how_to_fix: Create a Conditional Access policy requiring phishing-resistant MFA for all admin roles (or enforce via PIM activation), and block legacy auth. Re-check the policy applies to every privileged role.

items.title: Conditional Access policies
items.description: Block legacy auth, require compliant devices, and restrict risky sign-ins.
items.how_to_find:
List CA policies and look for gaps (no baseline, disabled policies, missing legacy-auth block):

```
az rest --method GET --url "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies" --query "value[].{name:displayName,state:state}"
```

No enforcing policies / everything in report-only = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Deploy baseline CA policies: require MFA for all users/admins, block legacy authentication, require compliant/hybrid-joined devices for sensitive apps, and restrict risky sign-ins. Move policies from report-only to enabled after testing.

items.title: Guest user access restricted
items.description: Limit external collaboration and review guest permissions regularly.
items.how_to_find:
Enumerate guest (B2B) users and their access:

```
az ad user list --query "[?userType=='Guest'].{upn:userPrincipalName,mail:mail}" -o table
```

Guests with broad roles or stale accounts are findings.
items.how_to_fix: Set external collaboration settings to least privilege (restrict guest permissions), require approval for invites, review guests via Access Reviews, and remove stale guests. Re-audit guest access.

items.title: Service principal credential rotation
items.description: Rotate client secrets and certificates; prefer managed identities.
items.how_to_find:
Find service principals with old/long-lived secrets or certs:

```
az ad app list --query "[].{name:displayName,appId:appId}" -o table
az ad app credential list --id <appId> --query "[].{end:endDateTime,type:keyId}"
```

Secrets past rotation age (or non-expiring) = medium finding.
items.how_to_fix: Rotate SP credentials regularly, prefer certificates or workload identity federation (OIDC) over client secrets, set short expiry, and remove unused credentials/apps. Re-check credential ages.

items.title: PIM for just-in-time admin
items.description: Use Privileged Identity Management for standing admin access.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether admin roles are permanently assigned vs eligible via PIM:

```
az rest --method GET --url "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/roleManagement/directory/roleAssignmentSchedules" -o json
```

Standing (permanent) privileged assignments = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Move privileged roles to PIM eligible assignments with time-bound activation, approval, and MFA; remove permanent admin assignments. Enable alerts on activations. Re-verify no standing admin.

items.title: NSG rules not over-permissive
items.description: No 0.0.0.0/0 on RDP, SSH, SQL, or management ports.
items.how_to_find:
Find NSG rules allowing management/data ports from Internet:

```
az network nsg list --query "[].{name:name,rg:resourceGroup}" -o table
az network nsg rule list --nsg-name <nsg> -g <rg> --query "[?access=='Allow' && sourceAddressPrefix=='*' && (destinationPortRange=='22' || destinationPortRange=='3389')]"
```

`*`/`Internet` source to 22/3389/DB ports = critical.
items.how_to_fix: Restrict inbound rules to specific source prefixes (bastion/VPN), remove `*`/`Internet` sources for management/data ports, use Azure Bastion for RDP/SSH, and apply default-deny. Re-scan NSGs.

items.title: VNet flow logs enabled
items.description: Enable NSG flow logs and traffic analytics for visibility.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether NSG/VNet flow logs are enabled:

```
az network watcher flow-log list --location <region> -o table
```

No flow logs = finding (no network forensics).
items.how_to_fix: Enable NSG/VNet flow logs to a Storage account + Log Analytics (Traffic Analytics), set retention, and monitor. Re-verify flow logs are on for all networks.

items.title: Private endpoints for PaaS
items.description: Use private link for storage, SQL, and Key Vault where possible.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether PaaS services (Storage, SQL, Key Vault) use private endpoints vs public access:

```
az network private-endpoint list --query "[].{name:name,rg:resourceGroup}" -o table
az storage account show -n <acct> --query 'publicNetworkAccess'
```
items.how_to_fix: Deploy Private Endpoints for PaaS services and set `publicNetworkAccess=Disabled`, use private DNS zones, and restrict to the VNet. Re-verify services are only privately reachable.

items.title: Azure Firewall or WAF in front of apps
items.description: Protect public-facing apps with WAF and centralized egress control.
items.how_to_find:
Check for a WAF/Firewall protecting public apps:

```
az network application-gateway waf-policy list -o table
az network front-door waf-policy list -o table 2>/dev/null
```

Public web apps without WAF = high finding.
items.how_to_fix: Front public apps with Application Gateway WAF or Front Door WAF (Prevention mode, managed rulesets + rate limiting), and route all ingress through it. Egress via Azure Firewall. Re-verify WAF is in Prevention mode.

items.title: DDoS Protection Standard
items.description: Enable on production VNets serving internet-facing workloads.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether DDoS Protection Standard is enabled on VNets with public endpoints:

```
az network vnet list --query "[].{name:name,ddos:enableDdosProtection}" -o table
```
items.how_to_fix: Enable DDoS Network Protection on VNets hosting public-facing resources, configure alerts, and combine with WAF/rate limiting. Re-verify protection is enabled where public IPs exist.

items.title: Storage account public access disabled
items.description: Disable anonymous blob access and review network rules.
items.how_to_find:
Check for public blob access and open network rules:

```
az storage account list --query "[].{name:name,pub:allowBlobPublicAccess,net:networkRuleSet.defaultAction,https:enableHttpsTrafficOnly}" -o table
```

`allowBlobPublicAccess=true` or `defaultAction=Allow` = high finding.
items.how_to_fix: Set `allowBlobPublicAccess=false`, `defaultAction=Deny` with specific VNet/IP rules (or private endpoints), and `enableHttpsTrafficOnly=true`. Audit containers for anonymous access. Re-check settings.

items.title: Encryption at rest with CMK
items.description: Use customer-managed keys for storage and databases where required.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether storage/disks use customer-managed keys vs Microsoft-managed:

```
az storage account show -n <acct> --query 'encryption.keySource'
```

`Microsoft.Storage` where CMK is required is a finding.
items.how_to_fix: Configure CMK encryption via Key Vault (`Microsoft.Keyvault` key source) with a managed identity granted key access, and enable auto-rotation. Re-verify key source is Key Vault.

items.title: SQL TDE and auditing enabled
items.description: Transparent encryption plus audit logs to Log Analytics.
items.how_to_find:
Check Transparent Data Encryption and auditing on Azure SQL:

```
az sql db tde show --server <srv> --database <db> -g <rg> --query status
az sql server audit-policy show --name <srv> -g <rg> --query state
```

TDE off / auditing disabled = high finding.
items.how_to_fix: Enable TDE (ideally with CMK), enable server/database auditing to Log Analytics/Storage, and enable Microsoft Defender for SQL. Re-verify TDE and auditing are enabled.

items.title: SQL firewall not open to internet
items.description: Restrict database access to known IPs or private endpoints.
items.how_to_find:
Check SQL server firewall rules for `0.0.0.0` / Allow-Azure-Services + broad ranges:

```
az sql server firewall-rule list --server <srv> -g <rg> -o table
```

`0.0.0.0-255.255.255.255` or wide ranges = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Remove internet-wide firewall rules, use Private Endpoints + `publicNetworkAccess=Disabled`, and allow only specific IPs/VNets. Disable "Allow Azure services" unless required. Re-check rules.

items.title: Soft delete and versioning on storage
items.description: Protect against ransomware and accidental deletion.
items.how_to_find:
Check blob soft delete and versioning (protects against deletion/ransomware):

```
az storage account blob-service-properties show --account-name <acct> --query "{softDelete:deleteRetentionPolicy.enabled,versioning:isVersioningEnabled}"
```
items.how_to_fix: Enable blob + container soft delete with adequate retention, enable versioning, and consider immutable (WORM) policies for critical data. Re-verify soft delete and versioning are on.

items.title: VM disk encryption enabled
items.description: Azure Disk Encryption or server-side encryption for all VMs.
items.how_to_find:
Check OS/data disk encryption on VMs:

```
az vm encryption show --name <vm> -g <rg> 2>/dev/null
az disk list --query "[?encryption.type=='EncryptionAtRestWithPlatformKey'].name" -o table
```
items.how_to_fix: Enable Azure Disk Encryption / encryption-at-host, prefer CMK for sensitive workloads, and enforce via Azure Policy. Re-verify all disks are encrypted.

items.title: AKS RBAC and private cluster
items.description: Enable Azure RBAC, private API server, and network policies.
items.how_to_find:
Check AKS for RBAC/Entra integration and a private API server:

```
az aks show -n <cluster> -g <rg> --query "{rbac:enableRbac,aad:aadProfile,private:apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster}"
```

RBAC off / public API server = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Enable Azure RBAC + Entra integration, deploy as a private cluster (or restrict API server authorized IP ranges), enable network policy, and use managed identity. Re-verify RBAC + private API.

items.title: Managed identity over secrets
items.description: Apps and functions use managed identities instead of stored creds.
items.how_to_find:
Find apps/VMs using stored credentials instead of managed identity:

```
az webapp identity show --name <app> -g <rg> 2>/dev/null
az vm identity show --name <vm> -g <rg> 2>/dev/null
```

No managed identity + secrets in config = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Assign system/user-assigned managed identities and grant them least-privilege RBAC to resources (Key Vault, Storage), removing stored credentials/connection strings. Re-verify identities are used.

items.title: Defender for Cloud enabled
items.description: Turn on Defender plans for servers, containers, and databases.
items.how_to_find:
Check Defender for Cloud plan coverage:

```
az security pricing list --query "value[].{name:name,tier:pricingTier}" -o table
```

`Free` tier on key resource types = finding.
items.how_to_fix: Enable Defender for Cloud (Standard) plans for Servers, Storage, SQL, Containers, Key Vault, etc. across subscriptions, and act on Secure Score recommendations. Re-verify plans are Standard.

items.title: Patch management baseline
items.description: Use Update Management or Automanage for timely patching.
items.how_to_find:
Check update/patch compliance via Update Manager/Defender:

```
az vm assess-patches --name <vm> -g <rg> 2>/dev/null
```

Missing critical/security updates = medium finding.
items.how_to_fix: Enable Azure Update Manager with scheduled patching + maintenance windows, enforce via policy, and track compliance. Prioritize critical/security patches. Re-assess after patching.

items.title: Activity Log to Log Analytics
items.description: Centralize subscription activity and resource logs.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether the subscription Activity Log is exported to Log Analytics:

```
az monitor diagnostic-settings subscription list --query "value[].{name:name,ws:workspaceId}" -o table
```

No export = critical (no central audit trail).
items.how_to_fix: Create a subscription diagnostic setting exporting the Activity Log (all categories) to a central Log Analytics workspace (and immutable storage), with retention per compliance. Re-verify export exists.

items.title: Diagnostic settings on all resources
items.description: Export metrics and logs for critical resources.
items.how_to_find:
Identify resources lacking diagnostic settings (no resource-level logs):

```
az monitor diagnostic-settings list --resource <resource-id> -o table
```

Key resources (Key Vault, NSG, SQL, Storage, App Gateway) without diagnostics = critical.
items.how_to_fix: Enable diagnostic settings on all sensitive resource types to Log Analytics, enforce via Azure Policy (`DeployIfNotExists`), and set retention. Re-verify coverage across resources.

items.title: Key Vault purge protection
items.description: Enable soft delete and purge protection on production vaults.
items.how_to_find:
Check soft-delete + purge protection on Key Vaults:

```
az keyvault list --query "[].{name:name,softDelete:properties.enableSoftDelete,purge:properties.enablePurgeProtection}" -o table
```

Purge protection off = finding (keys/secrets permanently deletable).
items.how_to_fix: Enable soft-delete and purge protection on all Key Vaults, restrict access with RBAC + private endpoints, and enable logging. Re-verify purge protection is on.

items.title: Key rotation policy
items.description: Automate rotation for keys and secrets in Key Vault.
items.how_to_find:
Check whether Key Vault keys have a rotation policy:

```
az keyvault key rotation-policy show --vault-name <kv> --name <key> 2>/dev/null
```

No rotation policy on long-lived keys = medium finding.
items.how_to_fix: Set automatic rotation policies on Key Vault keys, define lifetime actions, and monitor expiry. Re-verify rotation policies exist for CMKs.

items.title: Alert rules for security events
items.description: Alert on failed logins, key deletions, and NSG changes.
items.how_to_find:
Check for alert rules on security-relevant events (role changes, NSG changes, Key Vault access, sign-in risk):

```
az monitor activity-log alert list -o table
az monitor scheduled-query list -o table 2>/dev/null
```
items.how_to_fix: Create alert rules (Activity Log + Log Analytics/KQL) for privileged role changes, NSG/firewall changes, Key Vault secret access, and risky sign-ins, routed to an action group (email/ITSM/on-call). Test the alerts fire.
